annotations.label: The category Et(X)

anchor.snippet: the category Et( X ) is the category of all étale morphisms from a scheme to X

annotations.note: `Scheme.Etale X` is defined as `MorphismProperty.Over @Etale ⊤ X`, the category of schemes étale over `X`.

annotations.label: Étale presheaf

anchor.snippet: we define an étale presheaf on a scheme X to be a contravariant functor from Et( X ) to sets

annotations.note: No dedicated `étalePresheaf` definition, but presheaves on the étale category are obtained via general `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type` infrastructure over `Scheme.Etale X`.

annotations.label: Étale sheaf

anchor.snippet: an étale presheaf is called a sheaf if it satisfies the same condition

annotations.note: Étale sheaves can be expressed as `Sheaf (smallEtaleTopology X) _` via the generic `CategoryTheory.Sheaf` framework; no dedicated named abbreviation.

annotations.label: Étale cohomology groups

anchor.snippet: The étale cohomology groups H i ( F ) of the sheaf F of abelian groups are defined as the right derived functors of the functor of sections

annotations.note: General sheaf cohomology `Sheaf.H F n` is defined as an `Ext` group, which specializes to étale cohomology when applied to `smallEtaleTopology X`; no étale-specific wrapper exists.

annotations.label: Right derived functors R^q f_*

anchor.snippet: a morphism of schemes f : X → Y induces a map f ∗ from étale sheaves over X to étale sheaves over Y , and its right derived functors are denoted by R q f ∗

annotations.note: No étale (or general site) higher direct image `R^q f_*` construction is set up; only general `rightDerivedFunctor` infrastructure exists.

annotations.label: Finite locally constant sheaf

anchor.snippet: An abelian étale sheaf F over X is called finite locally constant if it is represented by an étale cover of X

annotations.note: No `LocallyConstantSheaf` or finite-locally-constant étale sheaf definition was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Constructible sheaf

anchor.snippet: It is called constructible if X can be covered by a finite family of subschemes on each of which the restriction of F is finite locally constant

annotations.note: No `ConstructibleSheaf` definition exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Torsion sheaf

anchor.snippet: It is called torsion if F ( U ) is a torsion group for all étale covers U of X

annotations.note: No `TorsionSheaf` definition was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hierarchy: locally constant ⊂ constructible ⊂ torsion

anchor.snippet: Finite locally constant sheaves are constructible, and constructible sheaves are torsion

annotations.note: None of the three sheaf classes is defined in Mathlib, so the inclusions are not stated.

annotations.label: Torsion sheaf as filtered colimit of constructibles

anchor.snippet: Every torsion sheaf is a filtered inductive limit of constructible sheaves

annotations.note: Not present; torsion and constructible étale sheaves are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: ℓ-adic cohomology group

anchor.snippet: and defines the ℓ-adic cohomology group

annotations.note: `EllAdicCohomology X ℓ n` is defined as the cohomology of the `ℤ_[ℓ]`-coefficient sheaf on the pro-étale site, the modern equivalent of the inverse-limit definition.

annotations.label: ℓ-adic sheaf

anchor.snippet: An ℓ-adic sheaf is a special sort of inverse system of étale sheaves

annotations.note: `ellAdicSheaf X ℓ` is the sheaf `U ↦ C(U, ℤ_[ℓ])` on the pro-étale site, the pro-étale incarnation of an ℓ-adic sheaf.

annotations.label: H^1 of curve is dual to Tate module of Jacobian

anchor.snippet: When V is a non-singular algebraic curve of genus g , H 1 is a free Z ℓ -module of rank 2 g , dual to the Tate module of the Jacobian variety of V

annotations.note: No Tate module, Jacobian variety, or H¹-duality theorem is in Mathlib (the `Jacobian` files concern Jacobian models of elliptic curves).

annotations.label: H^1(X, G_m) is the Picard group

anchor.section: Calculation of H 1 ( X , G m )
anchor.snippet: In particular H 1 ( X , G m ) is the Picard group Pic( X )

annotations.note: The étale sheaf G_m and the identification H¹(X, G_m) ≅ Pic(X) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vanishing of H^i(X, G_m) for i ≥ 2

anchor.section: Calculation of H i ( X , G m )
anchor.snippet: so all the cohomology groups H i ( X , G m ) vanish if i ≥ 2

annotations.note: Higher G_m cohomology vanishing is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Computation of H^i(X, μ_n)

anchor.section: Calculation of H i ( X , μ n )
anchor.snippet: If μ n is the sheaf of n -th roots of unity and n and the characteristic of the field k are coprime integers

annotations.note: No étale sheaf μ_n nor its cohomology computations are formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Computation of H^i(X, Z/nZ)

anchor.section: Calculation of H i ( X , Z/ n Z)
anchor.snippet: The étale group H i ( X , Z / n Z ) is then a free module over the ring Z / n Z and its rank is given by

annotations.note: Étale cohomology with ℤ/nℤ coefficients and rank formulas are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Étale cohomology of Spec K equals Galois cohomology

anchor.snippet: If X is the spectrum of a field K with absolute Galois group G , then étale sheaves over X correspond to continuous sets

annotations.note: The equivalence between étale sheaves on Spec K and continuous G-sets / Galois cohomology is not formalized.

annotations.label: Complex variety: étale = singular cohomology with finite coefficients

anchor.snippet: If X is a complex variety, then étale cohomology with finite coefficients is isomorphic to singular cohomology with finite coefficients

annotations.note: The comparison theorem between étale and singular cohomology of complex varieties is not formalized.

annotations.label: Coherent sheaves: étale cohomology = Serre's

anchor.snippet: If F is a coherent sheaf (or G m ) then the étale cohomology of F is the same as Serre's coherent sheaf cohomology calculated with the Zariski topology

annotations.note: The agreement of étale and Zariski cohomology on coherent sheaves is not formalized.

annotations.label: ℓ-adic cohomology of abelian varieties and curves

anchor.snippet: For abelian varieties and curves there is an elementary description of ℓ-adic cohomology

annotations.note: No description of ℓ-adic cohomology of abelian varieties or curves via Tate modules is present in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: The étale cohomology groups with compact support of a variety X are defined to be

annotations.note: Compactly-supported étale cohomology is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Higher direct images with compact support R^q f_!

anchor.section: Poincaré duality and cohomology with compact support
anchor.snippet: the higher direct images with compact support R q f ! are defined by

annotations.note: `R^q f_!` for étale sheaves is not formalized.

annotations.label: Poincaré duality for étale cohomology

anchor.section: Poincaré duality and cohomology with compact support
anchor.snippet: This is the analogue of Poincaré duality for étale cohomology

annotations.note: Poincaré duality for étale cohomology is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Point count formula for a curve over F_p

anchor.snippet: Let X be a curve of genus g defined over F p , the finite field with p elements

annotations.note: The point-count formula |X(𝔽_p)| = 1 + p − Tr(Frob | H¹) for curves over 𝔽_p is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lefschetz fixed-point formula

anchor.snippet: According to the Lefschetz fixed-point theorem , the number of fixed points of any morphism f : X → X is equal to the sum

annotations.note: The Lefschetz fixed-point formula (étale or topological form) is not present in Mathlib.
